page: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations placing in the top 10%. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, though single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the average tier.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation, where it achieves top-tier scores. It also shows strong capability in LLM inference prompt processing, though text generation speeds for larger models remain average due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The absence of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for I/O-heavy database workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 128 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying on a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong single-core execution. This profile makes the instance highly effective for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications, though workloads requiring high local disk I/O or GPU acceleration will face architectural tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 AMD EPYC cores, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 AMD EPYC cores, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and memory-intensive computing.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9R14 cores running at 3.7 GHz
1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and software compilation
